description: Sector overlay for South African manufacturing, processing, assembly, product safety, quality, compulsory specifications, legal metrology, and regulated-product documentation.

metadata.scope: South Africa-only sector overlay controls for Manufacturing. Intended to be used with south_africa_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official South African statutes, regulators, guidance, and sector sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.

primary_sources.name: National Regulator for Compulsory Specifications Act, 2008 and NRCS compulsory specifications

rules.title: NRCS compulsory specification evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for regulated-product compulsory specification, VC, NRCS approval, or conformity evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: SANS or quality-standard evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for South African National Standard, SANS, ISO/SANS, quality-control, or product-standard references.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Legal metrology or trade measurement evidence is present

metadata.review_guidance: A qualified South African legal/compliance reviewer must confirm applicability, current-law accuracy, legal sufficiency, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

source_basis: Consumer/product safety expectations under NRCS and consumer protection frameworks
